title: We are Hiring: Communication & Projects Assistant (Apprenticeship)
content:
We are hiring an apprentice Communication & Projects Assistant (Apprenticeship/Alternance only) to join our team in Paris, starting October 2026.

MCA is a European non-profit network dedicated to promoting digital cultural heritage, working with over 200 partner organisations across Europe. As part of a team based in Paris and Italy, you’ll help create content for our social media and website, produce podcasts and videos, and support the organisation of events — including our annual forum — within our European projects.

We’re looking for a Master’s (M2) student in communication or cultural project management, fluent in French and English, with a good grasp of tools like Canva, Adobe Suite, Premiere/iMovie, and WordPress.

📄 Full job offer attached below — including all responsibilities, required skills, and practical details.

  • French: Offre d’alternance
  • English: Job offer

    • Application deadline: 15 August 2026
    Interviews: 1–15 September
    Apply: send your CV, cover letter and portfolio to contact@michael-culture.eu

    The post We are Hiring: Communication & Projects Assistant (Apprenticeship) appeared first on Michael Culture Association.

title: Meet our 4 New Members!
content:
Last June, we just got a little bigger, and a lot more diverse. We’re thrilled to welcome four new members to our network! Different countries, different disciplines, different scales, but all working, in their own way, at the intersection of culture, technology, and people.

Here’s a closer look at who they are.

Laboratorio Lapsus is a non-profit organisation working at the crossroads of historical research, participatory history, education and community engagement. Its mission: making history a living, shared practice rather than something told to communities. Lapsus builds public history and educational projects that make people the active protagonists of their own history.

Their work spans oral histories of marginalised and underrepresented communities, critical nodes of contemporary history, and public narratives, always approached with historiographical rigour, but through collaborative, participatory and creative methods. Lapsus coordinates and takes part in European projects, weaving transnational partnerships across the cultural and third sector, with the aim of fostering critical thinking, civic engagement, and a deeper sense of belonging to shared cultural heritage.
